title: Instructional Goals and Objectives (Local Policy)
description: This policy establishes requirements for how Conroe ISD teachers make their instructional plans or course syllabi available to students and families. It applies to teachers and administrators across the district, as well as the students and parents who rely on that information. This policy matters to parents and community members because it helps ensure families can stay informed about what is being taught in the classroom and what to expect throughout the school year.

timeline_local.summary: This is a new local policy (EA Local) for Conroe ISD establishing requirements for the availability of instructional plans or course syllabi. It requires the District to post syllabi on its website at the beginning of each semester, requires teachers to provide copies to district administration and parents of enrolled students, and requires teachers to make additional copies available to parents upon request. The entire policy appears to be newly added text (no prior language existed).
changes_added: At the beginning of each semester, the District shall post on its website an instructional plan or course syllabus for each class offered in the District that semester.

In order for the District to make this required posting, each teacher shall provide a copy of the instructional plan or course syllabus for each class for which the teacher provides instruction to: 1. District administration; and 2. The parent of each student enrolled in the class.

Additionally, each teacher shall provide for additional copies of the instructional plan or course syllabus for each class for which the teacher provides instruction to be made available to a parent of a student enrolled in the class on the parent's request.

This is labeled 'Update: 1' indicating it is the first local policy update for EA. Page 144 of the meeting packet; Policy Update Page Number 7.

description_local: This policy establishes requirements for how Conroe ISD teachers document and share their instructional plans or course syllabi, ensuring that lesson goals and learning objectives are available to those who need them. It applies to teachers and school staff responsible for planning and delivering instruction across the district. For parents and community members, this policy matters because it supports transparency in the classroom, making it easier to understand what students are expected to learn and how teachers are planning to teach it.
